SPACEStat allows quantitative assessment at single-cell level or per-slide, focusing on spatial metrics

Spatial analysis of 20 tCycIF samples

Study of spatial distribution on single-cell level reveals distinct spatial patterns

Metrics to quantify whole-slide distribution of cells

Study of spatial distribution on single-cell level reveals distinct spatial patterns

Study of spatial distribution on single-cell level reveals distinct spatial patterns

Immune infiltration index

Macrophage infiltration patterns

Motivation of network analysis for immune ecosystems

Network analysis

Cells connected using distance threshold (here15 microns)

Connected components filtered with cell count threshold 10

Network of connected components

Previously collected connected components of the single cell networks are now connected using distance threshold (here 300 microns)

Each point represents group of > 10 cells

Community detection using Leiden algorithm

The network is partitioned into communities by maximizing a quality function called modularity

Algorithm aims to maximize connections within communities while minimizing connections between communities

Clustering of communities

Community clusters of 20 samples

Network results - clusters & their association with clinicomolecular features

Cluster composition - immune hubs

Relative abundance of cell communities

Cluster 7 negatively correlate with HRD scores

Cluster composition - immune hubs

Relative abundance of cell communities

Example 1
Correlation with HRD scores, tumor mutational burden and relative abundance of each cluster

Spearman correlation

Example 2 Principal component analysis based on immune composition & its association with HRP status

Principal component 1 is driven by cluster 7 composed of mostly CD8 T cells hubs in stroma

Loading score - influence of each cluster on principal component

Cluster 7 composition of hubs

Samples on the far right of the PCA plot are enriched for Cluster 7

Parameter tuning and method validation

Parameters:
What distance thresholds to use when connecting cells? 
What cell count threshold to use to filter connected components? 

Validation:
Does the method catch the real patterns in the data?
Does it produce “random” emergent patterns?

SIMULATED DATA

Step 1: Defining the tissue

Step 2: Removing voids

Step 3: Define tumor area

Area specific cell type type densities

Whole tissue

Stroma

Tumor

Three types of simulations

CSR (complete spatial randomness):

All cell types generated independently

All cells placed randomly using poisson point process

Clustering amongst the cells of same type (based on Thomas process) :

Three cell type specific parameters:

Clustering probabilities
Cluster size ranges
Cluster spreads

Some cells become centers of clusters based on the clustering probabilities

There is possibility of nested clustering
(cluster inside cluster)

Interacting cell clusters:

Each cell type has specified affinity (probability score) towards other cells

When cell becomes a center of a cluster, the probabilities are used to determine the cell types of the cells in the cluster.

Observed

CSR

No visible interactions

Clustering with Thomas process

CD11 (blue) clustered in stroma and CD8 (orange)
clustered in tumor

Interacting clusters

Parameter tuning with simulated data: Number of connected components

Parameter tuning with simulated data: Number of connected components

Strategies communities and ROIs

Validation of network: Does it correctly detect clustering?

Metric tested: Clustering coefficient

= how many of that node’s neighbors are also connected to each other, compared to the maximum number of connections they could have

Two simulations

Image: Ferraz de Arruda, Guilherme & Rodrigues, Francisco & Moreno, Yamir. (2018). Fundamentals of spreading processes in single and multilayer complex networks. Physics Reports. 756. 10.1016/j.physrep.2018.06.007.

CSR:

-All cells placed independently

Simulated clustering

-Bcells, CD8, CD4: Clustering in stroma

CD11: Clustering in Tumor

Macrophages: No clustering (identical to CSR)

Second test: Number of connected components larger than the set threshold (10 cells)

No connected components over the threshold in CRS sample!
